name: Mustard Pork Chop in Foil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Tear heavy-duty foil into a 12-inch square and spread oil evenly on it.
3. Place onion slice on half of the foil.
4. Trim excess fat from the pork chop, then spread mustard on both sides.
5. Place the pork chop on top of the onion and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Add rosemary if desired.
6. Fold the foil to form a triangle shape, rolling the edges and crimping firmly to seal. Make sure the sealed edges are facing upward (a significant amount of juices will be generated during cooking).
7. Place the foil packet in a baking pan to catch any juices that may leak.
8. Bake for 20-30 minutes, or until the pork chop is no longer pink in the center.
9. Carefully remove the foil packet and turn the chop with onion and juices onto a dinner plate.
10. Serve with baked potato or sweet potato if desired.
Note: Each serving contains 219 calories (41% from protein, 5% from carbohydrate, 54% from fat), 22 grams of protein, 3 grams of carbohydrate, 13 grams of fat, 74 milligrams of cholesterol, and 325 milligrams of sodium. Exchange values: 1/2 vegetable, 3 1/2 meat.
